rules.name: Direct Echo of User Input
rules.description: User input echoed directly to output without HTML escaping
rules.recommendation: Always escape output with htmlspecialchars() or htmlentities() before displaying user-controlled data
remediationSteps: Use htmlspecialchars() for HTML context
Use htmlentities() for complete HTML encoding
Specify encoding: ENT_QUOTES and UTF-8
Consider using templating engines with auto-escaping (Twig, Blade)

rules.name: Reflected XSS via Query Parameters
rules.description: User input from URL parameters reflected in HTML without sanitization
rules.recommendation: Validate and sanitize all query parameters before displaying them
remediationSteps: Whitelist expected parameters and validate format
Use htmlspecialchars() with ENT_QUOTES flag
Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers
Use security headers: X-XSS-Protection, X-Content-Type-Options

rules.name: Short Echo Tag with User Input
rules.description: Short echo tag <?= ?> outputs user input without escaping
rules.recommendation: Use full echo tag with htmlspecialchars() or disable short echo tags if not needed
remediationSteps: Replace <?= ?> with <?php echo htmlspecialchars(...); ?>
Ensure short_open_tag is properly configured in php.ini
Use a templating engine that auto-escapes by default

rules.name: JavaScript innerHTML with User Input
rules.description: DOM innerHTML property set with unsanitized user input in JavaScript
rules.recommendation: Use textContent instead of innerHTML, or sanitize with DOMPurify library
remediationSteps: Use textContent or innerText for plain text (safer)
Use DOMPurify library if HTML output is necessary
Use Framework-specific templating (React, Vue auto-escape)
Never use innerHTML with user input directly

rules.name: Event Handler Injection
rules.description: User input injected into event handler attributes (onclick, onload, etc.)
rules.recommendation: Always escape user input in event handlers or use addEventListener in JavaScript
remediationSteps: Escape user input in event handler attributes
Use addEventListener() instead of inline event handlers
Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) to prevent inline scripts

rules.name: Stored XSS via Database
rules.description: Unsanitized user input stored in database and executed later
rules.vulnerableExample: $comment = $_POST['comment']; mysqli_query($conn, "INSERT INTO comments VALUES ('$comment')"); // then later: echo $comment;
rules.secureExample: $stmt = $conn->prepare("INSERT INTO comments VALUES (?)"); $stmt->bind_param('s', $_POST['comment']); $stmt->execute(); // then later: echo htmlspecialchars($comment);
rules.recommendation: Use prepared statements for database operations AND escape output when displaying
remediationSteps: Use prepared statements for data insertion
Validate and sanitize input on storage
ALWAYS escape output when retrieving from database (defense in depth)
Implement input validation whitelist

rules.name: String Interpolation in HTML
rules.description: String interpolation or variable embedding in HTML without escaping
rules.secureExample:
const html = `

${escapeHtml(userInput)}

`; // or use React/Vue framework
rules.recommendation: Use framework templating with auto-escaping or implement escaping function
remediationSteps: Use React, Vue, or framework with auto-escaping
Implement custom escapeHtml() function
Never build HTML strings manually
Use template libraries with auto-escaping (e.g., Handlebars)

rules.name: Script Tag Injection
rules.description: User input used to create or modify script tags
rules.secureExample: // Don't include user input in script src. Use whitelisted scripts only or use data attributes
rules.recommendation: Never include user input in script tags. Use static script references or API endpoints
remediationSteps: Never include user input in script src attribute
Use API endpoints for dynamic content loading
Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) with strict script-src
Use subresource integrity (SRI) for external scripts

rules.name: SVG/Vector Graphics Injection
rules.description: User input injected into SVG or vector graphics with event handlers
rules.secureExample: Use DOMPurify to sanitize SVG or serve user SVGs as static files only
rules.recommendation: Sanitize SVG content with DOMPurify or serve user SVGs as static assets with proper headers
remediationSteps: Use DOMPurify library for SVG sanitization
Set Content-Type: image/svg+xml header for static SVG files
Implement CSP with img-src and object-src directives

rules.name: URL Scheme Injection (javascript:)
rules.description: User input in href or src attributes allows javascript: protocol execution
rules.secureExample: Click // or validate URL scheme
rules.recommendation: Validate URLs to ensure safe protocols (http, https, mailto) and escape output
remediationSteps: Whitelist allowed URL schemes (http, https, mailto, tel)
Use filter_var() with FILTER_VALIDATE_URL
Escape output with htmlspecialchars()
Implement CSP with frame-src and script-src directives

rules.name: DOM-based XSS via window.location
rules.description: JavaScript uses URL parameters without sanitization
rules.recommendation: Use textContent instead of innerHTML for URL parameter display or sanitize with DOMPurify
remediationSteps: Use textContent or innerText instead of innerHTML
Sanitize with DOMPurify library
Validate URL parameters server-side
Use Framework-based routing to prevent direct URL manipulation

rules.name: CSS Injection
rules.description: User input in style attributes can cause visual manipulation or data exfiltration
rules.secureExample:
Content
// or use class-based styling
rules.recommendation: Avoid user input in style attributes. Use CSS classes and validation instead
remediationSteps: Use predefined CSS classes instead of inline styles
If user styles needed, use CSS property whitelist (color, font-size only)
Implement Content Security Policy with style-src directive
Never allow user input to define CSS selectors or @import rules
